title: The Window Is Twelve Years Wide. After That, the Damage Is Done.
description: A landmark 2026 study mapped PFAS exposure onto a precise developmental timeline — and found that children re-exposed between ages eight and twelve show measurably lower bone density, with effect sizes rivaling diet, exercise, and genetics. With 158 million Americans drinking PFAS-contaminated water, this is a population-scale skeletal health crisis that won't show up as fractures for another thirty years. The study has limits. The implications don't.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Window Is Twelve Years Wide. After That, the Damage Is Done.
